questions.question: Explain the physical structure of a hard disk drive. Define the terms: platter, track, sector, and cylinder.
keyPoints: Platter: circular disc coated with magnetic material; has two surfaces
Track: concentric circle on a platter surface
Sector: subdivision of a track; smallest physical storage unit (512 bytes or 4 KB)
Cylinder: set of all tracks at the same position across all platter surfaces
All heads move together; data within a cylinder needs no head movement

questions.question: Explain Logical Block Addressing (LBA). How does the disk controller map a logical block number to a physical (cylinder, head, sector) address?
keyPoints: LBA treats disk as a 1D array of blocks numbered 0 to N-1
OS uses block numbers; controller handles physical mapping
Mapping order: sectors first, then heads, then cylinders
Formula: Cylinder = B/(S×H), Head = (B/S) mod H, Sector = B mod S
Where S = sectors per track, H = number of heads, B = logical block number
Provide a worked numerical example
Advantages over CHS: simpler for OS, portable, no geometry dependency

questions.question: Differentiate between Constant Angular Velocity (CAV) and Constant Linear Velocity (CLV) with examples.
keyPoints: CAV: constant RPM, same sectors per track, outer tracks waste capacity
Used by HDDs; simpler design
CLV: variable RPM, outer tracks have more sectors, uniform data density
Used by CD-ROMs, DVDs, Blu-ray
Zone Bit Recording (ZBR) is a hybrid used by modern HDDs

questions.question: Compare HDD and SSD in terms of structure, performance, and disk scheduling applicability.
keyPoints: HDD: magnetic platters, moving heads, seek time 3-15 ms
SSD: NAND flash, no moving parts, ~0.1 ms access time
HDD benefits from disk scheduling (SSTF, SCAN) to minimize head movement
SSD: scheduling algorithms irrelevant because all locations accessed equally fast
SSD has limited write cycles; requires wear leveling

questions.question: A disk has 10,000 cylinders, 8 heads, 200 sectors per track, and 512 bytes per sector. Calculate the total capacity. Also, find the physical address of logical block 5000.
keyPoints: For block 5000: S=200, H=8
Sector = 5000 mod 200 = 0
Block 5000 → Cylinder 3, Head 1, Sector 0
